solo_female_override_note: Christmas-NY week packs the crescent with stag tourism; return after Jan 5 for the usual solo-safe rhythm.
Peak charter-tourism week — stag harassment + beach-shack fights documented. Move to South Goa (Agonda/Palolem) entirely.
First week of January still absorbs NYE overflow; shifts safer after Jan 8.
Peak party-strip week; Anjuna flea + midnight raves = stag-group density. Stay in Vagator side only, skip Anjuna beach at night.
Peak Chronicle + Nine Bar crowd = aggressive stag flow. Stay in Vagator hotels with security; skip walking the cliff strip.
Even quiet Agonda fills with holiday-week bookings; beach-shack staff changes. Stick to named long-stay cottage operators.
Russian-New-Year crowd peaks; Russian-mafia-affiliated clubs in the area have documented harassment. Stay cottage-side, not club-strip.
Christmas sees a backpacker-density peak + Indian domestic stag flow; stay cliff-north, not the south-cliff extension.
Peak domestic-tourist male-stag week; Lighthouse Beach harassment reports spike. Stay at resort (Taj, Leela) with private beach.
Peak Western-backpacker week overlaps with Indian domestic crowd; Om + Kudle stay safe, Half Moon + far-north coves get sparse and risky.
Maha Kumbh + Magh Mela overlap years (2025 was Maha, 2028 Ardh) turn the city into 100M-pilgrim density; skip outside organised yatra format.
Mauni Amavasya + Magh Mela close means extreme density; railhead overcrowding is the documented solo-female hazard.
Kanwar Yatra peak — male-pilgrim density + road-aggression reported. Haridwar ghats remain policed; outskirts + NH roads need care.
Simhastha Kumbh years (next 2027) turn pilgrim density + transport chaos; Panchavati during Shravan is already crowded. Skip overnight solo this month.
Trimbakeshwar Shravan close — thin-the-herd tail end, pilgrim density still above comfort. Day-trip from Pune.
Rath Yatra period (late Jun–Jul) — 3M pilgrims in 3 days. Solo female infeasible unless part of an organised trust-booked group.
Rath Yatra + Sayana Ekadashi overlap month — ongoing density. Return Aug onwards.
Pushkar Camel Fair week (Kartik Purnima) — 2L visitors, stag-tour operators + photography-tourists. Skip or stay in a trust-booked haveli only.
Simhastha-year-adjacent months (next Simhastha 2028) + April Narmada Panchakroshi Yatra = pilgrim-density spike. Off-Simhastha, score returns to 3.
Bhadrapada Poornima mela = 5M pilgrims in a week. Temple trust manages accommodation only for registered yatra groups; solo infeasible.
Kapat-closing week — tail-end pilgrims, shrinking transport, early snow. Return in May when infrastructure restarts.

Mandalam-Makaravilakku peak pilgrim season (Nov 17 – Jan 14) — men-only cultural context strongest; women 10-50 face direct hostility. Skip absolutely.
Mandalam-Makaravilakku close — same context. Skip absolutely.
Peak summer holiday week (Jun 10-30) = Delhi/Punjab stag-group density at Old Manali + Mall. Book ahead + stay in New Manali with hotel security.
Monsoon thins the weekender stag flow — safer walk of Old Manali even solo.
Weekend monsoon crowd is entirely Mumbai/Pune stag groups — bhushi-dam-area incidents documented. Stay at Shillim/retreat properties, not Mall Road.
Continued monsoon stag-group density; same caveat as July.
Same Mumbai/Pune weekend-stag overlap as Lonavala. Stay Mahabaleshwar-side instead.
May weekend holiday crowd = family-heavy but stag-group overflow from Panchgani. Stay Club Mahabaleshwar, not Mapro-area lodges.

Winter Leh — flight-only access, minus-teens, thin tourist flow. Homestays (Omasila, Nimmu House) provide heated safety; still a committed trip, not casual.
Chadar-trek month brings international solo women; Leh town is otherwise quiet. Homestay-booked + flight-in only.
Late-winter Leh context continues; Srinagar road still closed.

Amarnath yatra base month — CRPF convoys + yatra-pilgrim density. Tourism outside yatra route is fine; plan around convoy days.
Peak winter + snowfall — Dal freezes, thin tourist flow. Houseboat with heater (Sukoon) is safe; walk-outs limited.
Amarnath yatra base — Pahalgam is packed with yatris + security. Stay at Heevan/Pahalgam Hotel, avoid Chandanwari end.
Post-Amarnath quiet returns by mid-Aug; best window before school-holiday season.
Ski-season peak (Dec-Feb) — organised operators thick on the ground, international tourists present; safest winter Kashmir destination.

44°C peak + pre-monsoon muggy = walker-incident reports spike. Return Oct-Mar.
45°C+ peak; daytime fort-climbing not advisable. Amer + Nahargarh tours operate pre-noon only.
Same extreme-heat context.
Desert-festival season + Camel Safari peak — organised operators thick. Day-trip from Jodhpur works in any month.
Hornbill Festival (Dec 1-10) — 2L visitors, organised Nagaland Tourism package + vetted homestays = safer than the annual baseline.
Ziro Music Festival (late Sep) — organised festival camp + long-stay female audience = safer than off-festival baseline.
Orange Festival (mid-Dec) — operator-organised only, festival infrastructure elevates the solo-safe window from the annual 2/5.
Monsoon peak — waterfall at maximum volume, tour-bus + family crowd density. Safer than the average month.
Monsoon peak — waterfall tourism density helps safety-in-numbers. Off-monsoon the falls thin and so does tourist flow.
Monsoon peak — jeep safari operates daily, tourism flow highest. Solo women routinely join mixed jeeps.
Firefly week (late May - mid Jun) — organised camps, mass tourism, documented safe format. Safer than the average month.
